questions.question: Explain the PAC learning framework. Define epsilon and delta parameters with an example.
keyPoints: PAC = Probably Approximately Correct
Epsilon: maximum acceptable error rate (accuracy parameter)
Delta: maximum failure probability (confidence parameter)
P(error(h) <= epsilon) >= 1 - delta
Concept is PAC-learnable if sample complexity is polynomial

questions.question: What is VC dimension? Explain shattering and show why the VC dimension of linear classifiers in 2D is 3.
keyPoints: VC dimension = largest set of points that H can shatter
Shattering: every possible labeling is achievable by some h in H
3 non-collinear points in 2D: all 8 labelings separable by a line
4 points: XOR configuration cannot be linearly separated
Therefore VC dimension = 3

questions.question: Differentiate between hypothesis space and version space with examples.
keyPoints: Hypothesis space H: all possible hypotheses the algorithm considers
Version space: subset of H consistent with all training data
Version space shrinks as more data arrives
Example: linear classifiers as H, version space narrows with each example

questions.question: State and explain the No Free Lunch theorem. What is its practical significance?
keyPoints: No single algorithm is best for all problems
Averaged over all target functions, all algorithms perform equally
Must try multiple algorithms and validate for specific problems
Justifies diverse algorithm study

questions.question: Explain inductive bias in machine learning. Give examples of inductive bias in at least three algorithms.
keyPoints: Inductive bias: assumptions for generalizing beyond training data
Without it, no generalization is possible
KNN: locality assumption (nearby points have similar labels)
Linear regression: linearity assumption
Naive Bayes: conditional feature independence
Decision trees: preference for shorter trees (Occam's Razor)
